title: ID-Delayed CatBoost
summary: Driver identity is very high-cardinality and train/test entity coverage differs, so the model may memorize historical driver behavior too early.
rationale: Driver identity is very high-cardinality and train/test entity coverage differs, so the model may memorize historical driver behavior too early. CatBoost exposes feature weights and first-feature-use penalties that can defer ID reliance until physics/context features explain most of the signal.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost variant with penalties or downweights on Driver, Race_Year, and other sparse identifiers, while leaving tyre/race-state variables unpenalized. Compare this specialist alone and in late fusion against the normal CatBoost/autogluon stack.
expected_effect: Should improve cold-start robustness for rare or shifted drivers/races and make leaderboard behavior less brittle.
risk: If identity carries strong stable signal, over-penalizing it can reduce CV and LB alike.
